action.hypothesis: `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual` clearly lost at `K=24`, so the next severe-stagnation move should test a different built-in soft-codebook family rather than continue grouped or scalar tuning. The best next hypothesis is that `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual` can outperform the plain two-stage soft-codebook baseline at the smaller frontier-relevant `K=24` slot by improving support selection without the instability seen in the routed family.
action.summary: Run one architecture-comparison experiment at `K=24` with `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed. Success criterion: beat the current `K=24` frontier anchor FVU (`0.09216673672199249`) and determine whether gated soft-codebook support is a better mid-K backbone than the current plain two-stage recipe.
action.self_review: This follows the severe-stagnation guidance by trying a fundamentally different existing architecture and keeping the recipe otherwise fixed. I chose `K=24` because smaller-K improvements are more valuable than further high-K work, and this slot remains owned by the plain family after grouped and routed variants failed to displace it. The main risk is that gating will be another selector variant that underperforms the simpler two-stage soft-codebook model in the mid-K regime.
notes_to_memory: Round 104 showed `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual` is not competitive enough at `K=24`, so grouped soft-codebook support should not be the next mid-K backbone candidate.
Round 105 should continue severe-stagnation architecture search with another existing soft-codebook family rather than more scalar exploitation.

Target `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=24` first because improving a smaller-K frontier slot is more valuable than additional high-K probing, and gating offers a distinct support-selection mechanism from the plain, grouped, and routed variants already tried.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual` wins at `K=24`, run the same fixed recipe at `K=32` to test whether gating helps across the mid-K band.
If it is close but slightly behind at `K=24`, consider one confirmatory run at `K=32` before discarding the gated soft-codebook family.
If it clearly loses at `K=24`, reject this gated branch for mid-K and move the next severe-stagnation round to another existing family with a more distinct inductive bias.

result.description: Run one architecture-comparison experiment at `K=24` with `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed. Success criterion: beat the current `K=24` frontier anchor FVU (`0.09216673672199249`) and determine whether gated soft-codebook support is a better mid-K backbone than the current plain two-stage recipe.
result.self_review: This follows the severe-stagnation guidance by trying a fundamentally different existing architecture and keeping the recipe otherwise fixed. I chose `K=24` because smaller-K improvements are more valuable than further high-K work, and this slot remains owned by the plain family after grouped and routed variants failed to displace it. The main risk is that gating will be another selector variant that underperforms the simpler two-stage soft-codebook model in the mid-K regime.
